title: Intense Sardinian Lamb Gnocchi
description: A midnight craving dish that blends smoky lamb shoulder with creamy potato gnocchi in a fragrant sauce, perfect for a comforting Sardinian dinner.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat and sauté minced garlic until fragrant, about one minute.
Add the ground lamb shoulder, breaking it apart, and cook until browned and no longer pink, about eight minutes.
Pour in the red wine to deglaze, scraping the browned bits, and simmer until the liquid reduces by half, about five minutes.
Stir in beef broth, rosemary, sage, and lemon zest, season with salt and pepper, and let the mixture simmer gently for ten minutes.
Meanwhile, mash the boiled potatoes until smooth, then mix in potato flour, rosemary, and sage until well combined.
Fold the lamb mixture into the potato mixture, adjust seasoning, and shape about two-inch portions into gnocchi-shaped pieces.
Boil a pot of salted water and drop the gnocchi; cook until they rise to the surface, then simmer for another two minutes and drain.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley, with a side of low-tamasic greens if desired.
